Create a short Manim animation for a neurodivergent middle-school learner. Concept: Balanced forces keep motion. Teach these exact points: Balanced forces keep motion: If forces are balanced and an object is at rest, it remains at rest. Key detail: If forces are balanced and an object is moving at a constant speed in a straight line, it continues to move at the same speed in the same straight line. Create a short Manim animation for a neurodivergent middle-school learner. Concept: Inertia and constant motion. Teach these exact points: Inertia and constant motion: An object stays at rest or in uniform motion in a straight line unless acted upon by an external net force. Key detail: An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion with the same speed and in the same direction unless acted upon by an unbalanced force. Key detail: An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion with the same speed and in the same direction unless acted upon by an unbalanced force. Use simple labels, large readable text, limited motion, and clear visual cause-and-effect. Avoid dense paragraphs. Include one calm recap frame at the end. Create a short Manim animation for a neurodivergent middle-school learner. Concept: Resultant Force Calculation. Teach these exact points: Resultant Force Calculation: When two forces act in the same direction, the resultant force is the sum of the individual forces. Key detail: When two forces act in opposite directions, the resultant force is the difference between the magnitudes of the two forces, and its direction is the same as the larger force. Key detail: When two forces act in the same direction, the resultant force is larger than either individual force, and it is calculated by adding their sizes together. Use simple labels, large readable text, limited motion, and clear visual cause-and-effect. Avoid dense paragraphs. Include one calm recap frame at the end. Adaptation needs: OpenDyslexic, chunk_size=2, low_text, max_180s, micro_chunks, break_cards, stealth_assessment.
